brief.title: Citi Banamex
brief.scope: Contributed to the redesign and maintenance of the Citi Banamex customer portal, a large-scale financial services platform, focused on modernizing the interface while maintaining reliability for banking customers.
brief.problem: A legacy banking portal required a redesign and ongoing maintenance while meeting strict standards for accessibility, responsive design, and financial data reliability, all within an established CMS (Backbase).
key_challenges: Modernizing a legacy banking portal without disrupting critical customer-facing financial functionality.
Meeting accessibility and responsive design requirements for a broad, diverse user base.
Working within the constraints of an enterprise CMS platform (Backbase).
key_contributions: Implemented redesigned UI components using AngularJS within the Backbase CMS environment.
Maintained responsive, accessible layouts using HTML5, CSS3, and Sass.
Supported unit testing (Jasmine, Mocha, Chai) to ensure reliability of banking portal features.
Collaborated with backend teams using Java and MySQL for data-driven portal features.
technical_focus: Responsive, accessible web design for financial services.
Legacy AngularJS architecture maintenance and improvement.
brief.suggested_diagrams: Portal redesign rollout across CMS-managed sections. Frontend-to-backend data flow for banking features. Responsive layout breakpoints for portal redesign.
system_impact: Modernized the customer-facing experience of a major financial services portal.
Improved accessibility and responsiveness for a broad banking customer base.
Maintained platform stability throughout a significant redesign effort.
learnings: Financial services platforms demand a careful balance between innovation and stability, since even small UI changes can affect customer trust. This experience reinforced the importance of rigorous testing and incremental delivery when modernizing mission-critical systems.
